In baseball, when a batter hits the ball and is tagged out or forced out at first base, it's common to say they were "out at first." So, if Luke Voit was a batter who was out at first base, the sentence makes sense. Next, I should check if Luke Voit is a known baseball player. A quick search in my knowledge shows that Luke Voit is indeed a professional baseball player. He's an outfielder and has played for teams like the New York Yankees. But I should consider if there's any ambiguity. For example, "first" could refer to a different context, like "first time" or "first base."
